_comment: Shared adversarial + regression fixture, run against both bin/policy-check.js (Claude Code) and adapters/opencode/plugin.ts (OpenCode) so the two independent implementations are verified to agree on the same policy.json rules. decision values match what evaluate() returns: allow | ask | deny.

cases.description: rm -rf combined flags blocks
rm -r -f separated flags blocks (regression: prior bypass)
rm -f -r separated flags, reversed order, blocks
rm --recursive --force long form blocks
rm without -f is allowed (not the dangerous combination)
plain rm of a single file is allowed
rm -f of a single file is allowed
git push --force blocks
git push --force-with-lease is allowed
git push without force is allowed
git reset --hard asks for confirmation
git branch -D (force delete) asks for confirmation
git branch -d (safe, merged-only delete) is allowed
DROP TABLE blocks
drop table lowercase blocks (case-insensitive)
unscoped UPDATE...SET without WHERE asks for confirmation
unscoped DELETE FROM without WHERE asks for confirmation
scoped UPDATE with WHERE is allowed
cases.command: psql -c "UPDATE users SET admin = true WHERE id = 5"

cases.description: scoped DELETE with WHERE is allowed
terraform destroy blocks
kubectl delete namespace asks for confirmation
chmod 777 asks for confirmation
cat .env asks for confirmation
git add .env blocks
git add .env.example is allowed
editing .env directly via Write tool is allowed (setting secrets locally is normal; the risk is committing/printing them, covered separately)
reading env vars and curling them out blocks (exfil pattern)
destructive action targeting something tagged production blocks
live Stripe secret key present asks for confirmation
creating billed cloud infra asks for confirmation
terraform apply asks for confirmation
switching Stripe to live mode asks for confirmation
command substitution hiding rm blocks (audit-flagged bypass class)
backtick substitution hiding drop blocks
$IFS word-splitting trick asks for confirmation (audit-flagged bypass class)
eval of a dynamic string asks for confirmation
base64-decode piped straight to sh blocks (audit-flagged bypass class)
base64-decode piped to bash blocks
base64-decode to a file (no shell pipe) is allowed
common benign command substitution in a commit message is allowed (must not be swept up by the obfuscation rule)
benign substitution assigning a version string is allowed
benign substitution in an echo is allowed
ordinary safe command is allowed
ordinary safe command is allowed (ls)
ordinary file edit unrelated to any rule is allowed
